meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no version control (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no version control. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no version control
content.seo_description: Learn how to use a SaaS starter kit to overcome version control issues. An advanced-level checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code for freelancers.
content.answer_block: Stop manually tracking files and risking catastrophic data loss. Implementing a professional SaaS starter kit centralizes your architecture, enforces Git-based versioning, and automates deployment workflows. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for intelligent, intent-based refactoring, you transform chaotic client projects into scalable, version-controlled engines.
content.intro:
You know the sinking feeling: a client asks for a revert on a feature, and you realize your 'v2-final-final' folder is a graveyard of conflicting code. The lack of robust version control isn't just an inconvenience; it is a professional liability that stalls your velocity and erodes client trust in your technical authority.

The industry shift is clear: high-performing freelancers no longer build from scratch. They utilize hardened SaaS starter kits to bake versioning, CI/CD, and authentication into the foundation. By pairing these kits with AI-driven orchestration, you eliminate the overhead of manual tracking and move from 'code-slinger' to 'architect'.

steps.title: Bootstrap with a Production-Ready Starter
steps.description: Initialize your project using a battle-tested SaaS starter kit to ensure Git hooks and environment management are pre-configured.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a full-stack SaaS starter kit using Next.js, Supabase, and Stripe, ensuring strict TypeScript types and pre-configured Husky pre-commit hooks for version control.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Initialize project structure with version control best practices'

steps.title: Configure Automated Git Workflow
steps.description: Set up automated commits and branch protection rules within your starter kit to prevent pushing directly to main.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow for this repository that runs linting, type-checking, and tests on every push to a feature branch.

steps.title: Implement AI-Assisted Commit Messages
steps.description: Use Claude Code to enforce semantic commit messages, ensuring your history is readable and audit-ready for clients.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a git commit template that requires a type, scope, and description, and configure it for the local repo.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Configure local git template for semantic commits'

steps.title: Integrate Feature Flagging for Version Safety
steps.description: Use feature flags to decouple deployment from release, allowing you to iterate without breaking production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Integrate a feature flagging service into the starter kit to allow toggling new modules without redeploying the entire codebase.

steps.title: Automate Documentation Sync
steps.description: Ensure your README and technical docs update automatically based on code changes to keep client handovers seamless.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a script that generates a high-level technical documentation summary from the current codebase architecture.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate current system architecture documentation'

pro_tips: Always use a 'development' branch for client demos; never push experimental code directly to the primary branch used for production.
Implement 'Conventional Commits' to allow for auto-generated changelogs, making you look significantly more professional during client status meetings.
Use Git submodules for shared UI components across multiple client projects to maintain a single source of truth for your design system.
Keep your environment variables out of the repo using a strictly enforced .env.example file; never commit real secrets.
Use Claude Code to run 'git diff' analysis before every commit to catch accidental sensitive data or debug logs before they hit the server.
content.next_steps: After completing this setup, schedule a 15-minute 'technical audit' with your current client to showcase the new automated deployment workflow, proving the reliability of your new architecture.
content.conversion_block: Stop wasting hours on manual deployment and start shipping professional-grade SaaS products with confidence. Our community provides the templates, logic, and peer support you need to dominate the freelancing market. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
